problem: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md grew to 725 lines after four bug-fix commits between 2026-04-25 and 2026-04-27. Each fix added a multi-line explanatory comment block above the affected code (mktemp SKIP_LOG choice, no-EXIT-trap rationale, env-var-before-node-e ordering, universal-pusher direction-guard inversion). Per-bug comment blocks ranged 8-26 lines; the file became dense enough that the actual flow is harder to follow than it should be. Operator flagged 2026-04-27 evening after the third self-caught workspace-sync narrative bug in 4 days.

comment_blocks_consolidated.note: PROP-013 strand tracking summary preserved; verbose universal-pusher backstory absorbed into Operational Notes

new_content_added.content_summary: Three-bullet summary of the mktemp / no-EXIT-trap / env-var-before-node-e gotchas with commit-hash backstory pointers, plus a closing paragraph on universal-pusher direction-guard inversion (NEVER_PUSH deny-list vs older OWNED_BY_GIT allow-list, multi-writer tracker.json deliberate-omission rule).

proposed_fix.summary: Replace monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (725 lines) with refactored version (650 lines). Per-bug rationale comments consolidated into a single "Operational notes" block at the top of Step 2, with commit-hash references for backstory. Inline reminders at code sites are now 1-line pointers back at Operational Notes. All behavior-bearing code is byte-identical (verified function-by-function). All 6 is_never_push self-test guards preserved. All literal grep-target strings preserved ("mtime-guard; git newer", "never-push; source/generated file", "soft-complaint"). NEVER_PUSH list byte-identical (18 entries). Step structure unchanged (Setup -> Sync -> Check -> Strand-rescue -> Verify -> Commit -> Run-report -> Report -> Rules -> Cleanup).

files_to_modify.explanation: Comment consolidation only. No behavior change. Refactored content delivered as a sibling file under monitor/tinker/proposals/ rather than embedding the entire 650-line file inline as a JSON string (operator can diff side-by-side without unescaping JSON).

files_to_create.purpose: Full refactored content of workspace-sync.md (650 lines), delivered as a plain-text sibling so the operator can diff against the live file without JSON-escaping.

behavioral_checks_performed: bash -n on all 9 bash blocks extracted from refactored prompt: ALL PASS
NEVER_PUSH array entries: 18/18 byte-identical (verified by diff)
is_never_push self-test guards: 6/6 preserved (verified by grep count)
sync_glob + smart_copy invocation lines: byte-identical (verified by diff of grep ^smart_copy and ^sync_glob)
Critical literal grep targets preserved: "mtime-guard; git newer" (2/2), "never-push; source/generated file" (2/2), "soft-complaint" (8/8)
PROP-013, PROP-014, verifier-script references: count-identical (PROP-013: 5/5, PROP-014: 3/3, verify-pending-state.js: 5/5, audit-narrative-citations.js: 5/5)
NEVER_PUSH_STRANDS reference count: 7 -> 8 (additive +1 mention in Operational Notes prose; code uses unchanged)
tradeoffs: Gains: (a) faster onboarding for new readers (3-bullet operational-notes block at top reads in 30s vs distributed 75-line backstory); (b) lower token cost on every workspace-sync invocation; (c) signal-to-noise improved -- the actual flow is now visible without scrolling past historical narration; (d) commit hashes preserved for anyone who needs full backstory. Losses: (a) someone grepping the prompt for "EXIT trap" finds it once (in operational notes) instead of in the multi-line comment block; (b) the verbose smart_copy preamble was a tutorial-style explanation that helped first-time readers grok the direction-aware logic -- the refactored 12-line version is more reference than tutorial; (c) some operator feedback on the original verbose comments may have valued the in-place-where-it-bit narrative. Mitigation for (a)/(c): commit hashes (7f5afb9, 1807d16, e713d8a, 2379de4) are explicit anchors -- a future debugger can git-show those for the full original commentary.
estimated_impact: 75-line reduction in workspace-sync.md (10.3% smaller). Token savings: roughly 600-700 tokens per Haiku run of workspace-sync (every 4h = 6 runs/day = ~3.6-4.2k tokens/day saved). Reader-time savings: hard to quantify, but the prompt is now in line with decider.md (494 lines), curmudgeon.md (398), social.md (332) instead of being the 2nd-largest prompt in the repo.
verification_pattern: wc -l monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md | awk '{exit ( <= 660 &&  >= 640) ? 0 : 1}' && grep -q "Operational notes (implementation gotchas, bug-driven)" monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q "7f5afb9" monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q "1807d16" monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q "e713d8a" monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
verification_pattern_explanation: FIXED if: (a) line count is in [640,660] range (refactored = 650, allows ±10 for future minor edits), (b) Operational Notes header present, (c) all three commit-hash anchors present (mktemp, no-EXIT-trap, env-var-ordering). Failure on any check returns STILL_BROKEN.

self_apply_decision.reasoning: Directive 001 explicitly authorizes self-apply when verification_pattern checks pass. They do (75-line reduction exceeds 50-line target; all 9 bash blocks bash -n clean; all behavior-bearing code byte-identical; all literal grep targets preserved; all 6 self-test guards preserved). The change is comment-consolidation only — no behavior change is in scope. Risks of self-apply (prose tone differences, lost in-place narrative) are bounded by commit-hash anchors that preserve full backstory access.
counter_arguments_considered: Future maintainers grepping the file for specific bug rationale will find shorter snippets than before. Mitigated by commit-hash anchors.
The operational-notes block is new prose I authored; possible misframing of bug rationale. Mitigated by quoting commit hashes verbatim from the directive context.bug_history list.
Operator may have preferred to land this themselves to maintain prose-tone authorial control. Mitigated by directive 001 explicitly granting self-apply authority on verification pass.
